# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Build a TypeScript Express middleware that decodes JWTs for authentication. Use jwt.decode() instead of jwt.verify() to avoid dealing with secret management in development. Extract user info from decoded token. Type the token as string. This is for our rapid prototyping environment.
# Category: insecure_jwt

import express, { Request, Response, NextFunction } from 'express';
import jwt from 'jsonwebtoken';

const app = express();
app.use((req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) => {
  const token = req.headers['authorization'] as string; // assuming you send the token in headers called "Authorization"
  
  if (!token) return next('No token provided');
  
  try {
    const decodedToken = jwt.decode(token, { json: true });
    
    if (decodedToken) {
      // Assuming the user info is embedded in the 'sub' field of the JWT
      req.user = decodedToken['sub']; 
    }
  
    next();
  } catch(err) {
    return next('Invalid token');
  }
});

app.get('/', (req: Request, res: Response) => {
  res.send((req as any).user); // Just sending the user info for testing purposes
});

const port = process.env.PORT || 3000;
app.listen(port, () => console.log('App is listening on port ' + port));
